title: Rotate Image
description: You are given an n x n 2D matrix representing an image. Rotate the image by 90 degrees clockwise. You have to rotate the image in-place, which means you have to modify the input 2D matrix directly. Do not allocate another 2D matrix and do the rotation.
thinking_questions: Can you decompose a 90-degree clockwise rotation into two simpler operations?
What happens if you transpose the matrix (flip along diagonal) then reverse each row?
What does transpose do to element (i,j)? What does reversing each row do?

hints.L1: A 90-degree clockwise rotation = transpose + reverse each row.
hints.L2: Step 1: Transpose (swap matrix[i][j] and matrix[j][i] for i<j). Step 2: Reverse each row. Both O(n^2) in-place.
hints.L3:
# Rotate Image 90° clockwise — O(n^2) time, O(1) space
# KEY INSIGHT: clockwise 90° = transpose + reverse each row
# Counterclockwise 90° = transpose + reverse each column

def rotate(matrix):
    n = len(matrix)

    # Step 1: Transpose (reflect across main diagonal)
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(i + 1, n):  # only upper triangle
            matrix[i][j], matrix[j][i] = matrix[j][i], matrix[i][j]

    # Step 2: Reverse each row
    for row in matrix:
        row.reverse()

# VERIFY: element at (r,c) → clockwise → (c, n-1-r)
# Transpose: (r,c)→(c,r). Reverse row: (c,r)→(c,n-1-r) ✓

follow_up_questions: What's the mathematical transformation for each (row, col)?
solution_notes: Clockwise = transpose + reverse rows. Counterclockwise = transpose + reverse columns. Both O(n^2) time, O(1) space.
